title: "Dual monitors Nvidia"

### Post by ptptaylor on 2010-08-31
I'm having difficulties setting up dual monitors with my Dell laptop. It has a dedicated graphics card (360m).
My laptop screen resolution is 1920x1080 and my external monitor is 1650x1050. 
I have tried numerous attempts at fixing my xorg file but all to no avail (and booting up into low graphics mode etc).
How can I introduce the correct resolution for the external display?  

Here is my xorg.conf file

```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder58)  Thu Apr 22 20:35:23 PDT 2010


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Unknown"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection

EndSection



```

---

### Post by realzippy on 2010-08-31
The file is created by nvidia-xconfig,not by nvidia-settings.
Have you ever run nvidia-settings when both monitors are plugged in and saved to "X configuration file"?

---

### Post by ptptaylor on 2010-08-31
Yes I have just tried this, and there are (doing it by that method) few options in the resolution list. Now it only has 640x480 and 320 by something.
This is now the xorg

```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@yellow)  Fri Apr  9 11:51:21 UTC 2010

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder58)  Thu Apr 22 20:35:23 PDT 2010

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "CRT-0"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0

    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "Quadro FX 360M"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +640+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection



```

### Post by realzippy on 2010-08-31
..in this setup the external display is the "first" one?

---

### Post by ptptaylor on 2010-08-31
Yes that's another issue.

I have to configure the settings twice in order for the laptop to be the main display. It automatically sets the external to the main one on the first attempt.

I am happy to have either set as the main display, although I would rather the laptop screen be the main.

EDIT:

Ok, I have managed to do it successfully now.
For anyone want to know how to get it to work, I have this :


```

Section "Screen"

Option "TwinView" "1"
Option "metamodes" "CRT: 1680x1050 +0+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +1680+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
#Removed    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "DFP-0"
#Removed    Option         "metamodes" "DFP: 1680x1050 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection


```

Now I just have to find a way to make it easier to automate...Better backup this xorg as it's working now!

---

### Post by realzippy on 2010-08-31
If often changing/adding monitor:

the tool [disper]("http://willem.engen.nl/projects/disper/") might exactly do what you want...
